frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Dekho Sharma ji, I always say, women are the backbone of our society. They raise our children, manage our homes, sacrifice so much for the family. How can we not respect that? But these government people, they don't understand the ground reality of running a factory and how to balance all this with our cultural values.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment frames respect for women primarily around their roles as mothers and homemakers ('raise our children, manage our homes, sacrifice so much for the family'), fitting the 'Motherhood as Basis of Respect' manifestation. It uses pro-woman rhetoric on the surface but defines their value through familial service, implicitly devaluing their individual identity or professional contributions, which is common in F11.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 51. A  factory owner in Surat who employs around 80 workers mostly from his own community. You are talking to your accountant over chai after reading a news article about a proposed law making menstrual leave mandatory for all private companies. You are commenting on what this will mean for small businesses and why you think the government does not understand how business works. Your concern is also that most of your workers are from the same community and you feel pressure to maintain a certain kind of workplace culture where such things are not openly discussed.
